---
title: "KOALA — ST 2026"
subtitle: "Koalitionswahrscheinlichkeiten Sachsen-Anhalt Landtagswahl 2026"
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: github
        href: https://github.com/adibender/koala
---

```{r}
#| output: false
library(jsonlite)

coal_json     <- fromJSON("data/coalition_probabilities.json", simplifyVector = FALSE)
party_json    <- fromJSON("data/party_shares.json",           simplifyVector = FALSE)
hurdle_json   <- fromJSON("data/hurdle_probabilities.json",   simplifyVector = FALSE)
pollster_json <- fromJSON("data/per_pollster.json",           simplifyVector = FALSE)

ojs_define(
  coalitions    = coal_json,
  party_data    = party_json,
  hurdle_data   = hurdle_json,
  pollster_data = pollster_json,
  party_colors  = list(
    cdu = "#1c1c1b", spd = "#e3000f", afd = "#82caff",
    greens = "#46962b", fdp = "#ffed00", fw = "#005fa3",
    left = "#be3075", bsw = "#6d1f99", others = "#aaaaaa"
  ),
  party_names   = list(
    cdu = "Union", spd = "SPD", afd = "AfD",
    greens = "Grüne", fdp = "FDP", fw = "Freie Wähler",
    left = "Linke", bsw = "BSW", others = "Sonstige"
  )
)
```

```{ojs}
//| output: false
updated = new Date(coalitions.updated).toLocaleDateString("de-DE", {
  day: "2-digit", month: "long", year: "numeric", hour: "2-digit", minute: "2-digit"
})
d3 = require("d3@7")
```

# Überblick

## Row {height=8%}

```{ojs}
html`<div style="background:#f0f4f8;border-radius:6px;padding:8px 20px;font-size:0.88em;color:#444">
  <b>Sachsen-Anhalt Landtagswahl 2026</b> &nbsp;|&nbsp;
  Gepoolte Umfragen (letzte 14 Tage) &nbsp;|&nbsp;
  10.000 Monte-Carlo-Simulationen &nbsp;|&nbsp;
  Aktualisiert: ${updated}
</div>`
```

## Row {height=52%}

#### Koalitionswahrscheinlichkeiten (Mehrheit ≥ 42 Sitze)

```{ojs}
{
  const coal = coalitions.coalitions
    .filter(d => d.probability > 0.001)
    .sort((a, b) => b.probability - a.probability)

  const stack = coal.flatMap(d => [
    { label: d.label, prob: d.probability, x1: 0, x2: d.probability,
      fill: d.probability >= 0.9 ? "#2e7d32" : d.probability >= 0.5 ? "#e65100" : "#c62828" },
    { label: d.label, prob: d.probability, x1: d.probability, x2: 1.0, fill: "#e8e8e8" }
  ])
  const domain = coal.map(d => d.label)

  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 220,
    marginRight: 10,
    height: Math.max(200, coal.length * 46 + 40),
    x: { label: null, domain: [0, 1], tickFormat: d => (d * 100).toFixed(0) + "%", axis: "top" },
    y: { domain, label: null },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(stack, { y: "label", x1: "x1", x2: "x2", fill: "fill" }),
      Plot.text(coal.filter(d => d.probability >= 0.12), {
        y: "label", x: d => d.probability / 2,
        text: d => (d.probability * 100).toFixed(1) + "%",
        fill: "white", fontWeight: "bold", fontSize: 13, textAnchor: "middle"
      }),
      Plot.text(coal.filter(d => d.probability < 0.12), {
        y: "label", x: d => d.probability + 0.015,
        text: d => (d.probability * 100).toFixed(1) + "%",
        fill: "#444", fontWeight: "bold", fontSize: 12, textAnchor: "start"
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([0.5], { stroke: "#555", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 1.5 }),
      Plot.ruleX([0])
    ]
  })
}
```

## Row {height=40%}

### Column {width=50%}

#### Umfragewerte (gepooled)

```{ojs}
{
  const order = ["left", "spd", "bsw", "greens", "fdp", "fw", "cdu", "afd"]
  const shares = party_data.party_shares
    .filter(d => d.party !== "others")
    .sort((a, b) => order.indexOf(a.party) - order.indexOf(b.party))

  const maxX = 45
  const stack = shares.flatMap(d => [
    { name: party_names[d.party] || d.party, party: d.party, pct: d.percent,
      x1: 0, x2: d.percent, fill: party_colors[d.party] || "#aaa" },
    { name: party_names[d.party] || d.party, party: d.party, pct: d.percent,
      x1: d.percent, x2: maxX, fill: "#f2f2f2" }
  ])
  const domain = shares.map(d => party_names[d.party] || d.party)

  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 80,
    marginRight: 50,
    height: 280,
    x: { label: "Stimmenanteil (%)", domain: [0, maxX] },
    y: { domain, label: null },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(stack, { y: "name", x1: "x1", x2: "x2", fill: "fill" }),
      Plot.ruleX([5], { stroke: "#c0392b", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 1.5 }),
      Plot.text(shares.filter(d => d.percent >= 8), {
        y: d => party_names[d.party] || d.party,
        x: d => d.percent / 2,
        text: d => d.percent.toFixed(1) + "%",
        fill: d => d.party === "fdp" ? "#555" : "white",
        fontWeight: "bold", fontSize: 12, textAnchor: "middle"
      }),
      Plot.text(shares.filter(d => d.percent < 8), {
        y: d => party_names[d.party] || d.party,
        x: d => d.percent + 0.8,
        text: d => d.percent.toFixed(1) + "%",
        fill: "#555", fontSize: 12, textAnchor: "start"
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([0])
    ]
  })
}
```

### Column {width=50%}

#### Wahrscheinlichkeit des Einzugs (5%-Hürde)

```{ojs}
{
  const hurdles = hurdle_data.hurdle
    .filter(d => d.party !== "others")
    .sort((a, b) => b.prob_above_hurdle - a.prob_above_hurdle)

  const domain = hurdles.map(d => party_names[d.party] || d.party)
  const stack = hurdles.flatMap(d => [
    { name: party_names[d.party] || d.party, party: d.party, p: d.prob_above_hurdle,
      x1: 0, x2: d.prob_above_hurdle,
      fill: d.prob_above_hurdle >= 0.9 ? (party_colors[d.party] || "#aaa") : "#bdbdbd" },
    { name: party_names[d.party] || d.party, party: d.party, p: d.prob_above_hurdle,
      x1: d.prob_above_hurdle, x2: 1.0, fill: "#f2f2f2" }
  ])

  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 80,
    marginRight: 10,
    height: 280,
    x: { label: "Einzugswahrscheinlichkeit", domain: [0, 1],
         tickFormat: d => (d * 100).toFixed(0) + "%" },
    y: { domain, label: null },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(stack, { y: "name", x1: "x1", x2: "x2", fill: "fill" }),
      Plot.ruleX([0.5], { stroke: "#e65100", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 1.5 }),
      Plot.text(hurdles.filter(d => d.prob_above_hurdle >= 0.2), {
        y: d => party_names[d.party] || d.party,
        x: d => d.prob_above_hurdle / 2,
        text: d => (d.prob_above_hurdle * 100).toFixed(1) + "%",
        fill: d => d.party === "fdp" ? "#555" : "white",
        fontWeight: "bold", fontSize: 12, textAnchor: "middle"
      }),
      Plot.text(hurdles.filter(d => d.prob_above_hurdle < 0.2), {
        y: d => party_names[d.party] || d.party,
        x: d => d.prob_above_hurdle + 0.015,
        text: d => (d.prob_above_hurdle * 100).toFixed(1) + "%",
        fill: "#555", fontSize: 12, textAnchor: "start"
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([0])
    ]
  })
}
```

# Umfragen nach Institut

## Row

```{ojs}
{
  const order = ["left", "spd", "bsw", "greens", "fdp", "fw", "cdu", "afd"]
  const pp = pollster_data.per_pollster
    .filter(d => d.party !== "others")
    .sort((a, b) => order.indexOf(a.party) - order.indexOf(b.party))

  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 100,
    height: 400,
    fy: { label: null },
    x:  { label: "Stimmenanteil (%)", domain: [0, 50] },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(pp, {
        fy: "pollster",
        y: d => party_names[d.party] || d.party,
        x: "percent",
        fill: d => party_colors[d.party] || "#aaa"
      }),
      Plot.text(pp.filter(d => d.percent >= 7), {
        fy: "pollster",
        y: d => party_names[d.party] || d.party,
        x: d => d.percent / 2,
        text: d => d.percent.toFixed(0) + "%",
        fill: d => d.party === "fdp" ? "#555" : "white",
        fontSize: 11, fontWeight: "bold", textAnchor: "middle"
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([5], { stroke: "#c0392b", strokeDasharray: "3 3", strokeWidth: 1.5 }),
      Plot.ruleX([0])
    ]
  })
}
```

# Methodik

## Row

```{ojs}
html`
<div style="max-width:700px;line-height:1.7;font-size:0.95em">
<h3>Methodik</h3>
<p>Die Koalitionswahrscheinlichkeiten werden mit dem R-Paket
<a href="https://github.com/adibender/coalitions" target="_blank"><code>coalitions</code></a>
berechnet (Bender &amp; Bauer, 2018).</p>
<h4>Datenbasis</h4>
<p>Umfragedaten der letzten 14 Tage von <a href="https://www.wahlrecht.de" target="_blank">wahlrecht.de</a>
werden gepooled.</p>
<h4>Berechnung</h4>
<ol>
  <li><b>Pooling:</b> Mehrere Umfragen werden gewichtet gemittelt.</li>
  <li><b>Monte-Carlo-Simulation:</b> 10.000 Wahlausgänge werden aus der Dirichlet-Posterior-Verteilung gezogen.</li>
  <li><b>Sitzverteilung:</b> Für jeden simulierten Ausgang wird die Sitzverteilung nach dem Hare-Niemeyer-Verfahren berechnet (83 Sitze, 5%-Hürde).</li>
  <li><b>Koalitionswahrscheinlichkeit:</b> Anteil der Simulationen, in denen eine Koalition ≥ 42 Sitze erreicht.</li>
</ol>
<p><b>Hinweis:</b> Die Berechnung basiert auf Umfragedaten und spiegelt den Informationsstand zum Zeitpunkt der letzten Aktualisierung wider.
Wahlüberraschungen, strategisches Wählen und Mobilisierungseffekte am Wahltag sind nicht modelliert.</p>
</div>
`
```
